details.formula: Highest level of evidence cited in impact claims

details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).

metrics.context_qualifier: Cumulative number of animals helped by Dharamsala Animal Rescue since its founding.
metrics.evidence_quote: Founded in 2008, Dharamsala Animal Rescue has been helping 1500 animals annually through their programs and community outreach.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of dogs vaccinated for rabies in 2025 as part of the End Rabies program.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Baseline is 2686 vaccinations in 2023-24, showing an increase in 2025.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of dogs sterilized in 2025 as part of the Animal Birth Control program.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Baseline is 1032 sterilizations in 2023-24, showing an increase in 2025.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of rabies vaccinations administered during the 11-day annual camp in 2025, a 13% increase over 2024.

counterfactual_baseline.description: 13% increase over 2024's vaccination numbers, indicating a baseline of 1646 vaccinations in 2024.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of adults and children reached in person by humane education programs in 2025.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Baseline is 330 individuals reached in 2023-24, showing a significant increase in 2025.

metrics.context_qualifier: Average number of animals helped annually by Dharamsala Animal Rescue since its founding in 2008.
metrics.evidence_quote: With only 9 full time staff members Dharamsala Animal Rescue is able to spay/neuter roughly 500 animals each year.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of animals rescued in 2025 through the Street Animal Rescue program.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Baseline is 475 rescues in 2023-24, showing a slight decrease in 2025.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of amputations prevented in 2025 due to a reduction in road accidents from street murals.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Baseline is the number of amputations before the mural program, which saw a reduction in road accidents.

metrics.evidence_quote: This represents a total 6 lives saved, and 10 less amputations.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of animal lives saved in 2025 due to a 17% reduction in hit and run cases from street murals.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Baseline is the number of lives lost to road accidents before the mural program, which saw a 13.5% reduction in deaths.

metrics.evidence_quote: This represents a total 6 lives saved, and 10 less amputations.

metrics.context_qualifier: Percentage increase in rabies vaccinations in 2025 due to increased community participation.
metrics.evidence_quote: Record Totals and Increased Community Participation allowing for a 7% increase in sterilizations and a 9% increase in rabies vaccinations.

metrics.context_qualifier: Percentage increase in sterilizations in 2025 due to increased community participation.
metrics.evidence_quote: Record Totals and Increased Community Participation allowing for a 7% increase in sterilizations and a 9% increase in rabies vaccinations.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of dogs adopted into forever homes in 2025, including local and international adoptions.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Baseline is 6 dogs adopted in 2023-24, showing a decrease in 2025.

significant_events.event_name: Annual Rabies Vaccination and Awareness Camp
significant_events.source_quote: The Camp started with an event for awareness and pet vaccination in Rakkar Village where DAR is located, supported by our village council.
significant_events.summary: Held an annual rabies vaccination and awareness camp in Rakkar Village, supported by the village council, administering 1,860 vaccinations.

significant_events.source_quote: Community Education is the DAR Difference reaching over 600 adults and children in person and thousands via radio and press.
significant_events.summary: Reached over 600 adults and children in person and thousands via radio/press to promote compassion and reduce conflict.

significant_events.event_name: Strategic Partnership with Humane World for Animals
significant_events.source_quote: 2025 also brought a new strategic partnership with Humane World for Animals (formerly Humane Society of the USA/International).
significant_events.summary: Formed a new partnership with Humane World for Animals for veterinary training, humane catching methods, and community outreach support.

significant_events.source_quote: Road Safety Murals = Safer Streets with a 17% reduction in hit and runs.
significant_events.summary: Expanded street mural program in 2025, adding two new murals to reduce hit and run cases and road accidents.

significant_events.source_quote: In July, we added Himani Negi to our DAR Team as Community Engagement Manager. This staffing investment helped us expand and connect with more than 636 adults and children in person and reach thousands more via radio shows and press coverage.
significant_events.summary: Hired Himani Negi as Community Engagement Manager in July 2025 to expand outreach and education programs.

significant_events.source_quote: Clinic/Shelter Upgrades. Thanks to you, we have a new ambulance, laptops, a cauterizer, and a biochemistry analyzer
significant_events.summary: Upgraded clinic and shelter with new ambulance, laptops, cauterizer, and biochemistry analyzer to improve care.

significant_events.event_name: Documentary Feature: Tails of the Himalayas
significant_events.source_quote: DAR is Featured in Tails of the Himalayas... of Dogs and Devotees Documentary, scheduled to be released this year.
significant_events.summary: DAR featured in a documentary 'Tails of the Himalayas...of Dogs and Devotees' to be released in 2026, raising awareness.

data.original_text: Not only do they provide care for the large stray dog population (an estimated 5000 in Dharamsala) but also to local pets and farm animals.
We visit schools and villages to educate them about rabies and compassion towards animals.
Target demographics: to end the human/street dog conflict. Direct beneficiaries per year: last year we helped 3951 animals. Geographic areas served: Dharamsala India.
Dharamsala Animal Rescue is constantly working to end human death caused by rabies through humane animal control, rescue work, adoption and education.
DAR has implemented a humane and effective program for creating a healthy sustained population of stray dogs which directly impacts community health.
We treat animals in critical condition at our clinic and recovery center. Car accidents, wire traps, illnesses, skin diseases, and maggot wounds are some of the issues we see.
Since that time, we have helped more than 18,500 animals.
Results: We have helped over 23,000 animals.
Founded in 2008, Dharamsala Animal Rescue has been helping 1500 animals annually through their programs and community outreach.
With only 9 full time staff members Dharamsala Animal Rescue is able to spay/neuter roughly 500 animals each year.
